title: Floor live user count at 1 on room join and remove 'online' text

requirements.summary: Floor the displayed live user count at a minimum of 1 when the local user is present in the chatroom to prevent showing 0
acceptanceCriteria: The live user count displayed in ChatArea never shows 0 while the user is inside a room
If the backend returns a count >= 1, that real count is shown without modification
If the backend returns 0 (e.g., heartbeat not yet responded), the UI displays 1 instead

file_operations.description: In the live user count display logic, apply Math.max(1, activeUserCount) to ensure the displayed count is never less than 1 when the user is viewing the room. This guarantees the count shows at least 1 (representing the current user) even if the backend heartbeat response hasn't arrived yet or temporarily returns 0.

requirements.summary: Fire an immediate heartbeat call to the backend as soon as the user enters a chatroom, without waiting for the first 45-second interval tick
acceptanceCriteria: A heartbeat is sent to the backend immediately on room entry (not waiting for the 45-second interval)
The periodic 45-second heartbeat continues to run as before
The live user count updates to the real backend value as soon as the immediate heartbeat response arrives

file_operations.description: Add an immediate invocation of the heartbeat mutation when the hook initializes or when the roomId changes, before setting up the 45-second interval. This ensures the backend receives a presence signal immediately upon room entry, allowing the live user count to sync quickly. The existing interval-based heartbeat logic remains unchanged.

requirements.summary: Remove the word 'online' from the live user count display in ChatArea
acceptanceCriteria: The text 'online' does not appear anywhere in the live user count display inside a chatroom
The count number itself is still displayed

file_operations.description: Locate the JSX rendering the live user count and remove any text node, string literal, or variable containing the word 'online'. Ensure the numeric count remains visible without any 'online' suffix or prefix.
